heading: Mitigating and aggravating factors to be considered in determining whether a sentence of death is justified
label: 3592 Mitigating and aggravating factors to be considered in determining whether a sentence of death is justified
notes: References in Text, referred to in subsec. children.heading: .—Impaired capacity
children.label: 1 .—Impaired capacity
children.text: The defendant’s capacity to appreciate the wrongfulness of the defendant’s conduct or to conform conduct to the requirements of law was significantly impaired, regardless of whether the capacity was so impaired as to constitute a defense to the charge.

children.heading: .—Duress
children.label: 2 .—Duress
children.text: The defendant was under unusual and substantial duress, regardless of whether the duress was of such a degree as to constitute a defense to the charge.

children.heading: .—Minor participation
children.label: 3 .—Minor participation
children.text: The defendant is punishable as a principal in the offense, which was committed by another, but the defendant’s participation was relatively minor, regardless of whether the participation was so minor as to constitute a defense to the charge.

children.heading: .—Equally culpable defendants
children.label: 4 .—Equally culpable defendants
children.text: Another defendant or defendants, equally culpable in the crime, will not be punished by death.

children.heading: .—No prior criminal record
children.label: 5 .—No prior criminal record
children.text: The defendant did not have a significant prior history of other criminal conduct.

children.heading: .—Disturbance
children.label: 6 .—Disturbance
children.text: The defendant committed the offense under severe mental or emotional disturbance.

children.heading: .—Victim’s consent
children.label: 7 .—Victim’s consent
children.text: The victim consented to the criminal conduct that resulted in the victim’s death.

children.heading: .—Other factors
children.label: 8 .—Other factors
children.text: Other factors in the defendant’s background, record, or character or any other circumstance of the offense that mitigate against imposition of the death sentence.

children.heading: .—Aggravating Factors for Espionage and Treason
children.label: b .—Aggravating Factors for Espionage and Treason

children.heading: .—Prior espionage or treason offense
children.label: 1 .—Prior espionage or treason offense
children.text: The defendant has previously been convicted of another offense involving espionage or treason for which a sentence of either life imprisonment or death was authorized by law.

children.heading: .—Grave risk to national security
children.label: 2 .—Grave risk to national security
children.text: In the commission of the offense the defendant knowingly created a grave risk of substantial danger to the national security.

children.heading: .—Grave risk of death
children.label: 3 .—Grave risk of death
children.text: In the commission of the offense the defendant knowingly created a grave risk of death to another person.

children.heading: .—Death during commission of another crime
children.label: 1 .—Death during commission of another crime
children.text: The death, or injury resulting in death, occurred during the commission or attempted commission of, or during the immediate flight from the commission of, an offense under section 32 (destruction of aircraft or aircraft facilities), section 33 (destruction of motor vehicles or motor vehicle facilities), section 37 (violence at international airports), section 351 (violence against Members of Congress, Cabinet officers, or Supreme Court Justices), an offense under section 751 (prisoners in custody of institution or officer), section 794 (gathering or delivering defense information to aid foreign government), section 844(d) (transportation of explosives in interstate commerce for certain purposes), section 844(f) (destruction of Government property by explosives), section 1118 (prisoners serving life term), section 1201 (kidnapping), section 844(i) (destruction of property affecting interstate commerce by explosives), section 1116 (killing or attempted killing of diplomats), section 1203 (hostage taking), section 1992 (wrecking trains), section 2245 (offenses resulting in death), section 2280 (maritime violence), section 2281 (maritime platform violence), section 2332 (terrorist acts abroad against United States nationals), section 2332a (use of weapons of mass destruction), or section 2381 (treason) of this title, or , United States Code (aircraft piracy).1section 46502 of title 49See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: .—Previous conviction of violent felony involving firearm
children.label: 2 .—Previous conviction of violent felony involving firearm
children.text: For any offense, other than an offense for which a sentence of death is sought on the basis of section 924(c), the defendant has previously been convicted of a Federal or State offense punishable by a term of imprisonment of more than 1 year, involving the use or attempted or threatened use of a firearm (as defined in section 921) against another person.

children.heading: .—Previous conviction of offense for which a sentence of death or life imprisonment was authorized
children.label: 3 .—Previous conviction of offense for which a sentence of death or life imprisonment was authorized
children.text: The defendant has previously been convicted of another Federal or State offense resulting in the death of a person, for which a sentence of life imprisonment or a sentence of death was authorized by statute.

children.heading: .—Previous conviction of other serious offenses
children.label: 4 .—Previous conviction of other serious offenses
children.text: The defendant has previously been convicted of 2 or more Federal or State offenses, punishable by a term of imprisonment of more than 1 year, committed on different occasions, involving the infliction of, or attempted infliction of, serious bodily injury or death upon another person.

children.heading: .—Grave risk of death to additional persons
children.label: 5 .—Grave risk of death to additional persons
children.text: The defendant, in the commission of the offense, or in escaping apprehension for the violation of the offense, knowingly created a grave risk of death to 1 or more persons in addition to the victim of the offense.

children.heading: .—Heinous, cruel, or depraved manner of committing offense
children.label: 6 .—Heinous, cruel, or depraved manner of committing offense
children.text: The defendant committed the offense in an especially heinous, cruel, or depraved manner in that it involved torture or serious physical abuse to the victim.

children.heading: .—Procurement of offense by payment
children.label: 7 .—Procurement of offense by payment
children.text: The defendant procured the commission of the offense by payment, or promise of payment, of anything of pecuniary value.

children.heading: .—Pecuniary gain
children.label: 8 .—Pecuniary gain
children.text: The defendant committed the offense as consideration for the receipt, or in the expectation of the receipt, of anything of pecuniary value.

children.heading: .—Substantial planning and premeditation
children.label: 9 .—Substantial planning and premeditation
children.text: The defendant committed the offense after substantial planning and premeditation to cause the death of a person or commit an act of terrorism.

children.heading: .—Conviction for two felony drug offenses
children.label: 10 .—Conviction for two felony drug offenses
children.text: The defendant has previously been convicted of 2 or more State or Federal offenses punishable by a term of imprisonment of more than one year, committed on different occasions, involving the distribution of a controlled substance.

children.heading: .—Vulnerability of victim
children.label: 11 .—Vulnerability of victim
children.text: The victim was particularly vulnerable due to old age, youth, or infirmity.

children.heading: .—Conviction for serious federal drug offenses
children.label: 12 .—Conviction for serious federal drug offenses
children.text: The defendant had previously been convicted of violating title II or III of the Comprehensive Drug Abuse Prevention and Control Act of 1970 for which a sentence of 5 or more years may be imposed or had previously been convicted of engaging in a continuing criminal enterprise.

children.heading: .—Continuing criminal enterprise involving drug sales to minors
children.label: 13 .—Continuing criminal enterprise involving drug sales to minors
children.text: The defendant committed the offense in the course of engaging in a continuing criminal enterprise in violation of section 408(c) of the Controlled Substances Act (), and that violation involved the distribution of drugs to persons under the age of 21 in violation of section 418 of that Act ().21 U.S.C. 848(c)21 U.S.C. 859

children.label: A
children.text: the President of the United States, the President-elect, the Vice President, the Vice President-elect, the Vice President-designate, or, if there is no Vice President, the officer next in order of succession to the office of the President of the United States, or any person who is acting as President under the Constitution and laws of the United States;

children.label: B
children.text: a chief of state, head of government, or the political equivalent, of a foreign nation;

children.label: C
children.text: a foreign official listed in section 1116(b)(3)(A), if the official is in the United States on official business; or

children.label: i
children.text: while he or she is engaged in the performance of his or her official duties;

children.label: ii
children.text: because of the performance of his or her official duties; or

children.label: iii
children.text: because of his or her status as a public servant.

children.heading: .—Prior conviction of sexual assault or child molestation
children.label: 15 .—Prior conviction of sexual assault or child molestation
children.text: In the case of an offense under chapter 109A (sexual abuse) or chapter 110 (sexual abuse of children), the defendant has previously been convicted of a crime of sexual assault or crime of child molestation.

children.heading: .—Multiple killings or attempted killings
children.label: 16 .—Multiple killings or attempted killings
children.text: The defendant intentionally killed or attempted to kill more than one person in a single criminal episode.

children.heading: .—Aggravating Factors for Drug Offense Death Penalty
children.label: d .—Aggravating Factors for Drug Offense Death Penalty

children.heading: .—Previous conviction of offense for which a sentence of death or life imprisonment was authorized
children.label: 1 .—Previous conviction of offense for which a sentence of death or life imprisonment was authorized
children.text: The defendant has previously been convicted of another Federal or State offense resulting in the death of a person, for which a sentence of life imprisonment or death was authorized by statute.

children.heading: .—Previous conviction of other serious offenses
children.label: 2 .—Previous conviction of other serious offenses
children.text: The defendant has previously been convicted of two or more Federal or State offenses, each punishable by a term of imprisonment of more than one year, committed on different occasions, involving the importation, manufacture, or distribution of a controlled substance (as defined in section 102 of the Controlled Substances Act ()) or the infliction of, or attempted infliction of, serious bodily injury or death upon another person.21 U.S.C. 802

children.heading: .—Previous serious drug felony conviction
children.label: 3 .—Previous serious drug felony conviction
children.text: The defendant has previously been convicted of another Federal or State offense involving the manufacture, distribution, importation, or possession of a controlled substance (as defined in section 102 of the Controlled Substances Act ()) for which a sentence of five or more years of imprisonment was authorized by statute.21 U.S.C. 802

children.heading: .—Use of firearm
children.label: 4 .—Use of firearm
children.text: In committing the offense, or in furtherance of a continuing criminal enterprise of which the offense was a part, the defendant used a firearm or knowingly directed, advised, authorized, or assisted another to use a firearm to threaten, intimidate, assault, or injure a person.

children.heading: .—Distribution to persons under 21
children.label: 5 .—Distribution to persons under 21
children.text: The offense, or a continuing criminal enterprise of which the offense was a part, involved conduct proscribed by section 418 of the Controlled Substances Act () which was committed directly by the defendant.21 U.S.C. 859

children.heading: .—Distribution near schools
children.label: 6 .—Distribution near schools
children.text: The offense, or a continuing criminal enterprise of which the offense was a part, involved conduct proscribed by section 419 of the Controlled Substances Act () which was committed directly by the defendant.21 U.S.C. 860

children.heading: .—Using minors in trafficking
children.label: 7 .—Using minors in trafficking
children.text: The offense, or a continuing criminal enterprise of which the offense was a part, involved conduct proscribed by section 420 of the Controlled Substances Act () which was committed directly by the defendant.21 U.S.C. 861

children.heading: .—Lethal adulterant
children.label: 8 .—Lethal adulterant
children.text: The offense involved the importation, manufacture, or distribution of a controlled substance (as defined in section 102 of the Controlled Substances Act ()), mixed with a potentially lethal adulterant, and the defendant was aware of the presence of the adulterant.21 U.S.C. 802
